reviewer: hand-reviewed (Opus) — headless first-visit run with real download + inference, multi-language verification via transformers.js 3.7.5, desktop+mobile screenshots, responsive matrix

rubric.evidence: On-page headless run (WASM, no GPU, fresh profile): clicked 'Download model (~339 MB)' → loader state 'ready' → the page auto-ran the German sample "Die Regierung hat heute ein neues Gesetz zur Klimapolitik verabschiedet." with the native template "In diesem Text geht es um {}." and ranked Politik 35.6% #1 (Technologie 21.6%, Wirtschaft 20.2%, Sport 12.0%, Kochen 10.6%), 1156 ms, 0 console errors. Independent transformers.js 3.7.5 harness confirmed Spanish "Me encanta cocinar pasta con tomate y albahaca fresca." → cocina 45.3% #1, and the raw sequence-classification logits are [1,3]. Real MoritzLaurer/mDeBERTa-v3-base-mnli-xnli zero-shot pipeline runs in models/multilingual-zero-shot/worker.js.
rubric.notes: Genuinely multilingual zero-shot: correct top label on German and Spanish premises against invented labels, no training. q8 rankings are correct; margins are a touch flatter than fp32 (disclosed in the at-a-glance).

rubric.evidence: Output shape matches the zero-shot-classification contract: pipeline returns { sequence, labels (sorted desc), scores (aligned) }. 'See inside' additionally runs one NLI pass per label and reads the raw 3-way logits — verified dims [1,3] with config id2label {0:entailment,1:neutral,2:contradiction} — rendered as a stacked entailment/neutral/contradiction bar per label with entailment highlighted.
rubric.notes: mDeBERTa's config lists entailment at index 0 (unlike the English DeBERTa page's contradiction-first order); renderNLI locates each class by name so the highlight is order-independent.

rubric.evidence: Desktop screenshot confirms the at-a-glance table: model id MoritzLaurer/mDeBERTa-v3-base-mnli-xnli linked to card, task zero-shot-classification, head 'entailment/neutral/contradiction', quantization q8, backend 'WebAssembly (no WebGPU needed)', size ~339 MB, license MIT (weights) + Apache-2.0 (runtime). Inference off the main thread via worker.js.
rubric.notes: Honest, complete disclosure including the large ~339 MB download (250k SentencePiece vocab) and the multilingual distinction from the English DeBERTa zero-shot page.

rubric.evidence: Fresh profile: loader state 'download-required', single action 'Download model (~339 MB)' — no silent auto-download of a 339 MB model. After the click the loader progressed to 'ready' and the page ran. Uses the shared createModelLoader on the index + all four use-case pages.
rubric.notes: Matches the auto-init contract: absent → labelled Download; a valid cached copy auto-inits with no button (shared loader inspects the cache).

rubric.evidence: role=status aria-live regions on every page; the loader announces checking→downloading (with %)→ready; a .status.err surfaces classification failures on the page; the Classify control validates 'at least two labels' and 'template must contain {}' with a readable message. First-visit run showed the download progress path.
rubric.notes: Offline-cached and partial-eviction paths are handled by the shared loader but were not separately driven here.

rubric.evidence: Real, labelled controls: 6 language sample chips (min-block-size 2.2rem), a dir=auto textarea, a labels input, an editable hypothesis-template input (the multilingual-specific control the English page lacks), a multi-label checkbox, and a Classify button. Practical page routes a 5-message mixed-language inbox; wild page does cross-lingual label transfer; multi-model chains NER + zero-shot. All native, keyboard-operable elements.
rubric.notes: A full driven keyboard-traversal pass (focus order + visible focus at both viewports) remains as an agent check.

rubric.evidence: On a fresh profile the scores area is empty and the loader shows the honest 'This model isn't on your device yet.' download state — nothing canned is shown as live output. All ranked scores and the entailment breakdown come from the on-device run.

conformance script assertions pass by construction: every button has a text name; no without alt (SVG data-URI favicon only). Score bars and NLI stacks are text + styled divs with title tooltips; output containers carry dir=auto so RTL (Arabic) and CJK render correctly. Live regions announce results.
rubric.notes: Followed accessible-error-announcement guidance for the failure path; css-layout logical properties keep RTL correct.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check.mjs: desktop (1280×800) and mobile (360×740 DPR3) both automated-clean — scrollWidth ≤ innerWidth (mobile overflow 0px measured in the on-page driver too), no clipped controls, console clean, network clean. Screenshots read (dark theme): _verify/multilingual-zero-shot-{desktop,mobile}.png. At-a-glance table and chip rows reflow.
rubric.notes: support flipped to ok after the driven matrix pass (both classes exercised: layout + real inference + screenshots).

rubric.evidence: All classification runs in a Web Worker (worker.js) off the main thread; the on-page run measured 1156 ms for the German sample without blocking the UI. Per-label NLI passes for the see-inside surface also run in the worker.
rubric.notes: Architecture is INP-friendly (inference + per-label NLI both off-thread); an explicit INP trace under rapid label edits would confirm.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy synchronous work off the main thread (workers) / yield so it doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — zero-shot inference and the per-label NLI see-inside passes run in models/multilingual-zero-shot/worker.js; the control UI stays responsive.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; on-page run 1156 ms with 0 console errors and no UI jank

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap + logical properties + minmax auto-fit grids to avoid horizontal overflow on narrow viewports.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — chip rows use flex-wrap; the practical inbox uses grid repeat(auto-fit, minmax(260px,1fr)); inputs are inline-size:100%; dir=auto handles RTL.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-mobile clean at 360×740; measured 0px horizontal overflow; _verify/multilingual-zero-shot-mobile.png

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce load/inference/validation failures in a live region the user can read, not only the console.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — failures and validation messages set a .status.err in a role=status aria-live region; the shared loader exposes a Retry path.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: runStatus role=status aria-live on every page; loader error/Retry state

openQuestions: q8 flattens the single-label softmax margins vs fp32 — is a native-language template (already supported) enough, or should the page nudge users toward it more strongly by defaulting the template per detected language?
The ~339 MB download is large for mobile data plans — worth a more prominent size warning before the first Download on a metered connection?
How well does cross-lingual label transfer (wild page) hold for low-resource label languages outside the XNLI 15?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass drilling every control on mobile+desktop (chips, template edits, multi-label, inbox routing, cross-lingual presets, multi-model) with keyboard traversal + focus-ring evidence.
Add a 'policy moderation' practical demo: score text against editable policy labels in many languages with a multi-label threshold.

summary: multilingual-zero-shot is a strong, honest, genuinely multilingual zero-shot demo: real mDeBERTa-v3-base-mnli-xnli classification via transformers.js in a Web Worker, verified on-page (German → Politik #1) and in a standalone harness (Spanish → cocina #1) with the real 3-way entailment head exposed. Accurate at-a-glance disclosure (including the large ~339 MB download and q8 margin caveat), honest absent-model Download state (no silent transfer), an editable per-language hypothesis template the English page lacks, clean mobile+desktop layout (0px overflow), and a four-rung ladder (basics → mixed-language inbox routing → cross-lingual label transfer → NER+zero-shot composition). Distinct from the built English DeBERTa zero-shot page by architecture (mDeBERTa-v3, 100-language SentencePiece) and multilingual capability. Revision 1.
